name: on frFrame
title: on frFrame
description: This event procedure is called after a valid data frame has been received in the specified slot and cycle.
If two valid data frames are received in the according slot (on channel A and on B) then the event procedure is called up twice (once for each frame).
An optional channel parameter for event filtering can be assigned to all handlers:
An optional qualifier can be used to assign the handler to a specific network:
The following program reacts on received frames and prints them in the Write Window.

availability: CANoe Measurement Setup / CANalyzer Analysis Branch
CANoe Test Setup for Test Modules
CANoe Test Setup for Test Units

row: The Rx time stamp that has been synchronized with the global time base in the computer (hardware synchronization's reference channel or computer system clock).The time stamp must be used if time relations should be regarded with events from other sources. Note The Time selector is not available when executing CAPL programs directly on an interface hardware (CAPL-on-Board). Timer unit: 10 microseconds, data type: dword Write-protected!
Note The Time selector is not available when executing CAPL programs directly on an interface hardware (CAPL-on-Board).
Note The Time selector is not available when executing CAPL programs directly on an interface hardware (CAPL-on-Board).

The Rx time stamp that has been synchronized with the global time base in the computer (hardware synchronization's reference channel or PC system clock).The time stamp must be used, if time relations should be regarded with events from other sources. This time stamp is also output in the Trace Window when receiving a frame. Timer unit: nanoseconds, data type: int64 Write-protected!
The application channel that the FlexRay interface determines, which received the frame. Write-protected!
Identifies the FlexRay channel of the CC. With 1 the frame was received on channel A, with 2 on channel B. Write-protected!
The frame was received in this slot. Write-protected!
The frame was received in this cycle. Write-protected!
Length of the present payload in 16 Bit words (data type: word). Write-protected!
byte(index), word(index), dword(index), qword(index), char(index), int(index), long(index), int64(index), <signal name>

Direct access to the payload/data of the frame.The number of valid data bytes is specified by the selector FR_PayloadLength.The index is always byte-oriented and counted from 0. Thus, dword(1) returns the double word from bytes 1...4 and not from bytes 4...7. If the event procedure was initialized via a symbolic name from the database, signal names can also be used directly as selectors for the data range. The raw value of the signal is retrieved. The physical value can be retrieved by <signal name>.Phys. Write-protected!

Provides more detailed status information from the frame header, if necessary. Note These bit masks values are only to be used to read the FR_Flags in the event procedure on frFrame. Possible values: Bit Mask Meaning 0x1 Null Frame 0x2 Payload valid = msg contains valid data 0x4 Frame set the sync bit. 0x8 Frame set the start-up bit. 0x10 Frame set the payload preamble bit. 0x20 Frame set the reserved bit. 0x40 Frame is faulty. 0x80 — 0x100 — 0x200 — 0x400 — 0x800 — 0x1000 — 0x2000 — 0x4000 The frame was sent/simulated. 0x8000 The frame was received in asynchronous mode. 0x10000 — 0x20000 — 0x40000 — 0x80000 — 0x100000 Frame is part of the dynamic segment. 0x400000 — All other bits of the flags are reserved. Write-protected!
Note These bit masks values are only to be used to read the FR_Flags in the event procedure on frFrame.

Additional status information about the reception buffer of the CC. Caution! The value is hardware dependent according to the selected interface type. The selected interface type can be determined by calling FRGetConfiguration. Bit Mask Vector Interfaces FlexCard Cyclone II 0x0001 Syntax Error (SERR) Syntax Error (SERR) 0x0002 Content Error (CERR) Content Error (CERR) 0x0004 Slot Boundary Violation (BVIOL) Slot Boundary Violation (BVIOL) 0x0008 Empty Slot (SLEMPTY) Empty Slot (SLEMPTY) 0x0010 Message Lost (MLOST) Message Lost (MLOST) 0x0020 Valid Frame (VAL) Valid Frame (VAL) 0x0040 Tx Conflict Hdr (TXCON) — 0x0080 Framing Error (FrmERR) — 0x0100 Header CRC Error (HdrERR) — 0x0200 Frame CRC Error (FrmCRC) — 0x0400 — — 0x0800 Tx Conflict (TXCONFLICT) Due to the bus physics a transmission conflict is not detected in any case. Available from CANoe 7.2. — 0x1000 Redundancy Error Dual channel frame with different payload or header flags detected. — 0x2000 Bus Error Spikes detected. — 0x4000 Unknown error — 0x8000 — — All other bits of the status are reserved. Write-protected!
Caution! The value is hardware dependent according to the selected interface type. The selected interface type can be determined by calling FRGetConfiguration.
